title: Public Registry - Canada.ca
content:
The Health Canada Pesticides Regulatory Directorate (PRD) is responsible for pesticide regulation in Canada. The public registry is where you can learn more about all pest control products registered for use in Canada.
- Access pesticide product information database
- Manage your pesticide applications via ePRS
Look up established MRLs by pesticide or food commodity.
Use our label search to find the latest requirements on using products correctly.

## Recent postings of consultations and decisions

### Canola oil, garlic oil, capsaicin, related capsaicinoids, and Captiva Prime (PRD2026-10)
Open consultation Posted: June 9, 2026

### Spiropidion, A20262 Insecticide (PRD2026-09)
Open Consultation Posted: June 5, 2026

### Etofenprox, RF2129 EC and RF2220 Premium Aerosol II-M Premise Spray (RD2026-14)
Decision Posted: June 4, 2026

### Triazole pesticides – Cumulative health risk scoping assessment, problem formulation and planned approach of analysis (REV2026-02)
Decision Posted: June 1, 2026

## What's included in the registry

### Public consultations
Access PRD consultation documents for proposed decisions.

### Decisions and updates
Access PRD decision documents and updates.

### Policies and guidelines
View documentation describing how pesticides are regulated in Canada.

### International harmonization activities
Learn how we work with other regulatory bodies around the world.

### Acts, regulations and MOUs
View relevant legislation, as well as Memoranda of Understanding with other parties.

### Pesticide product information database
Use this robust search tool to access PRD records related to pesticide regulation.

### Public engagement portal
Pose a question, report an incident, file a complaint, request to inspect confidential test data, file a notice of objection to request a reconsideration of decision, and comment on a PRD consultation document.

### Pest management advisory council reports and recommendations
View Pest Management Advisory Council meeting reports and recommendations to the Minister of Health.
